def compute_factor(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.Series:
    # Sort by (code, date) so rolling ops are per-code
    df = df.sort(['code', 'date'])

    # Cross-sectional rank of 1/close
    rank_inv_close = rank(1.0 / pl.col('close')).over('date')

    # adv20 = 20-day average volume per code
    adv20 = rolling_mean(pl.col('volume'), window=20)

    # Cross-sectional rank of (high - close)
    rank_high_close = rank(pl.col('high') - pl.col('close')).over('date')

    # sum(high, 5) / 5  ->  5-day rolling mean of high per code
    sum_high_5 = rolling_sum(pl.col('high'), window=5) / 5.0

    # delay(vwap, 5)
    delay_vwap_5 = delay(pl.col('vwap'), periods=5)

    # Part 1: (rank(1/close) * volume) / adv20
    part1 = (rank_inv_close * pl.col('volume')) / adv20

    # Part 2: (high * rank(high - close)) / (sum(high,5)/5)
    part2 = (pl.col('high') * rank_high_close) / sum_high_5

    # Product of the two parts
    combined = part1 * part2

    # Cross-sectional rank of (vwap - delay(vwap, 5))
    rank_vwap_diff = rank(pl.col('vwap') - delay_vwap_5).over('date')

    # Final factor
    factor = combined - rank_vwap_diff

    return df.select(factor).to_series()
